items.title: Just one rental in Australia affordable for JobSeekers like Jess

children.content: Anglicare Australia's latest look at rentals shows the private market effectively does not exist for poorer Australians.

gallery.altText: A young woman smiles while playing with her cat on a desk.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman smiles while playing with her cat on a desk.

items.title: Canberra speech pathologist convicted for illegally obtaining medication

children.content: A Canberra speech pathologist is convicted of using the details of people she knew, including parents of two of her patients, to illegally obtain prescription medication.

image.altText: A concrete building reading "Law Courts of the Australian Capital Territory", with Australian, ACT and Aboriginal flag.

items.title: Hopes new Canberra strata recommendations will dispel 'shonks' from industry

children.content: An ACT Legislative Assembly committee inquiry wants to see strata managers get mandatory licenses.

image.altText: From the ground up, a large brown brick apartment complex stands, with a cloudy blue sky backdrop.

items.title: Knife used in Canberra Hospital incident 'secreted' from ACT police in earlier search

children.content: The ACT's chief police officer says the knife produced by a Canberra Hospital patient that led to an hours-long security incident had not been detected by police during a search of the man earlier in the day.

gallery.altText: The entrance to a hospitals emergency room.

thumbnail.altText: The entrance to a hospitals emergency room.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: Illicit cigarettes, vapes seized in Canberra retailer raids

children.content: The ACT's chief police officer wants a sustained crackdown on the black market tobacco trade, as authorities swoop on six Canberra businesses.

image.altText: Boxes of illicit cigarettes stacked in a container.

items.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs amid 'financial pressures'

children.content: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

image.altText: An image of the backs of two Border Force officers, their organisation clearly shown by signs on their backs

items.title: How beer and AI are changing the course of Canberra's economic future

children.content: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

gallery.altText: A man stands and smiles, leaning on a bench in a large workshop space with buckets and gear behind him.

thumbnail.altText: A man stands and smiles, leaning on a bench in a large workshop space with buckets and gear behind him.

items.title: Apologetic insurer claims customers were not tricked in renewal notices

children.content: When the corporate regulator accused RACQ of misleading customers with insurance renewal notices, the insurer apologised. Now, it is defending the notices in court filings.

image.altText: Blue letters spelling RACQ at the top of a white building.

items.title: Rebel Wilson trial told of 'culturally inappropriate Indian outfit' post

children.content: Rebel Wilson tells a court several social media posts targeting the lead actor on her film The Deb are all true, on her final day of cross-examination in a defamation trial.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court April 30 for defamation trial

items.title: Blues out to rewrite State of Origin history in Newcastle series opener

children.content: The Blues are yet to enjoy success in Newcastle, a fact they are mindful of ahead of the first Women's State of Origin match against the Maroons.

gallery.altText: A NSW Blues player holds the ball while being tackled by a Maroons opponent during the 2025 Women's State of Origin series.

thumbnail.altText: A NSW Blues player holds the ball while being tackled by a Maroons opponent during the 2025 Women's State of Origin series.

items.title: Private school denies discouraging parents from telling victims about deepfake incident

children.content: The principal of a Tasmanian private school denies claims by two parents that the school ever tried to discourage them from telling their daughters they had been identified in AI deepfake images.

image.altText: School Signage saying "The Friends School"

items.title: 'Forever changed': Parents speak after children killed in house fire

children.content: A statement shared by the parents of two children killed in a Bowen Mountain house fire say their "family's world has been forever changed".

image.altText: Myla Summer-Lea Hughes smiling in a blue shirt, and River William Higgins smiling in a striped orange and blue shirt.

items.title: Artist who taught himself to paint after seeing Roy and HG portrait wins prize

children.content: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

gallery.altText: Portrait of Jacob Collins by Sean Layh; a man laying in a disheveled bed.

thumbnail.altText: Portrait of Jacob Collins by Sean Layh; a man laying in a disheveled bed.

items.title: Mining prohibited 'in perpetuity' from culturally significant reserve

children.content: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

image.altText: Red sand dunes

items.title: Bottle shops awash with illicit booze as police probe arson attacks

children.content: The National Drug Research Institute found 30 per cent of bottle shops it audited across different areas of Melbourne and regional Victoria were stocking illicit alcohol.

image.altText: Spirit bottles in an alcohol shop.

items.title: 20 years after the original, The Devil Wears Prada 2 has something to say

children.content: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

items.title: SA premier defends course development despite LIV Golf doubts

children.content: Peter Malinauskas compares spending $45 million to redevelop the North Adelaide golf course to similar funding for the AFL to play in the Barossa Valley, as media reports suggest Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund is preparing to pull financial support for LIV Golf.

items.title: The 'Jessica' tantrum trend explained

children.content: Whether it's at home or in the middle of a shopping centre, tantrums can test the patience of any parent or caregiver.

items.title: Fed-up residents 'leaving town' due to murky tap water

children.content: When residents of Narrandera, in southern NSW, turn on the tap, the water that comes out is sometimes so dirty it stains their clothes.

items.title: CFMEU delegates hired as health and safety representatives, inquiry hears

children.content: The commission of inquiry into the CFMEU in Queensland hears that contractors were pressured to hire delegates as safety representatives at a Cross River Rail site.

items.title: On-duty paramedic stabbed outside cafe in Melbourne's north

children.content: Police have arrested a man who allegedly stabbed an on-duty paramedic in the suburb of Reservoir, in Melbourne's north, before fleeing the scene.

children.content: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

children.content: Truck drivers say the fines for dangerous overtaking should match drink driving penalties due to the risk it poses to other road users

children.content: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee is on trial over a cocaine shipment into Newcastle.

items.title: From cereal to clothing, see how the price of everyday items has changed

children.content: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

items.title: Police charge man for allegedly attending businesses while nude

children.content: A Mount Gambier man is granted bail after he was allegedly nude while in businesses and on a busy main road three times in the past week.

items.title: NZ mosque attacker's appeal rejected in 78-page judgment

children.content: A New Zealand court declines an application by the Australian man who murdered 51 people at two mosques in 2019 to appeal his convictions.

image.altText: A sign showing the Court of Appeal is displayed above a set of clear glass doors on a building.

items.title: Trump's would-be assassin took selfie 'armed to the teeth', court told

items.title: Mark Latham ordered to pay $100k to MP he sexually harassed and vilified

children.content: The former One Nation MP has been found to have vilified openly gay politician Alex Greenwich in the second case centring around a dispute between the pair.

image.altText: a composite image of Alex Greenwich and Mark Latham

items.title: Accused double killer Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother to remain in custody

children.content: The mother of former police officer Beau Lamarre-Condon will spend at least two weeks in custody after she was charged over an alleged attempt to influence a key witness to change their evidence in his upcoming murder trial.

items.title: King visits 9/11 memorial as he tours New York
